Finishing Your Lil Scarf

Back to Crochet Workshop Resources main page

Resources

  • Purl Soho Crochet Basics – quick reference for most skills in this pattern
  • Wool and the Gang crochet tutorial library – videos on a range of crochet skills used in Wool and the Gang patterns

Helpful Videos

  • Learn crochet as a beginner
  • How to hold your hook
  • How to: Crochet Turning Chain
  • How to join a new ball of yarn – not a required skill, but helpful if you encounter a knot

Lil’ Scarf Pattern – with video links!

Get Started

  1. Make a slip knot, chain (ch) 10, then make chain stitches until your chain is 4 inches long.

    Note: For a wider scarf, make a longer chain. For a narrower scarf, make a shorter chain.

    • Slip knot tutorial
    • Chain stitch tutorial
  2. Starting in the 2nd chain from your hook, single crochet across the row.
    • Single crochet tutorial
  3. Work in rows until your scarf measures 4 inches from your starting chain.

Shape the Scarf

  1. Decrease row: Work single crochet decreases (Sc2tog) in the first 2 sts and the last 2 sts of the row. [8 sc] ( __ - 2 sc)
    • How to decrease in single crochet
  2. Sc in rows until your scarf is 10 inches long.
  3. Alternate decrease rows (see step 4) and normal rows until only 2 single crochet stitches remain.
  4. Single crochet the final two stitches together.
  5. Fasten off by cutting the yarn about 6 inches from your hook, then pull the final loop through.

Make the Second Half

  1. Single crochet into the other side of each foundation chain stitch.
  2. Decide on your preferred scarf size:
    • For a single wrap lil’ scarf (~30 inch scarf), follow steps 2–9.
    • For a double wrap lil’ scarf (~40 inch scarf), sc in rows until the scarf measures 14 inches from the foundation chain, then follow steps 4–11.

Create a Border

  1. With border yarn, ch 1, then single crochet evenly around the edges of the scarf, placing 3 sc at each pointed end.
    • How to add a single crochet border
  2. Fasten off (see step 8), then weave in your ends.
    • How to weave in ends
